insights.title: Avoid overusing send/receive nodes inside a mod chain — prefer the normal signal flow
insights.body: Using many send/receive nodes inside a modchain for summing or routing creates unnecessary complexity. The author recommends using the normal top-down signal flow as much as possible and only resorting to send/receive when genuinely needed (e.g. feedback loops). To sum multiple modulation sources into a single output, use a split node inside the mod chain with a peak node placed after it.
insights.author: Christoph Hart [author]

insights.title: Deleting modulating nodes can leave orphan connections on the target node
insights.body: When a node that is modulating another node is deleted, the connection can persist on the target node and prevent it from working normally. If a modulated parameter behaves unexpectedly after removing nodes from the graph, manually inspect and remove any remaining connections on the target.
insights.author: ustk [trusted]

insights.title: Range editor requires values to be defined on both source AND target for connections to work
insights.body: Since the new_layout / later develop versions, connections between modchain nodes require the parameter ranges (min/max) to be defined on both the source node and the target node. Editing only the source was sufficient in older builds but no longer works. Use SHIFT-click on the min/max fields in the range editor to set values. You can set min greater than max to invert the curve.
insights.author: Matt_SF [trusted]
